attacking_style: Attacks in final 1.5-2km on extreme altitude finishes above 2000m, preferring short explosive efforts over sustained long climbs. Operates from breakaways rather than pure GC group dynamics.
team_tactics: Often placed in breakaways to serve dual objectives (stage hunting + KOM points). Limited evidence of dedicated domestique support, suggesting independent opportunistic role rather than protected GC leader status.
terrain_preference: Excels on high-altitude finishes (2300m+) with steep gradients in final 2km. Struggles on long, gradual sustained climbs and exposed in technical sprint finales where positioning awareness lacks.
vulnerability: Cracks on sustained gradual ascents despite explosive climbing strength. Poor positioning awareness in technical sprint finales leads to crash involvement. Cannot follow elite accelerations (Evenepoel-level) on punchy climbs.
key_patterns: High-altitude native advantage decisive above 2000m, multiple wins at 2300m finish elevation
Attacks final 1.5km specifically on steep gradients, pattern repeated across multiple Giro stages
Operates from breakaways with split objectives: stage wins prioritized over KOM secondary goals
Exceptional mental resilience: recovered from 80km crash to win same stage demonstrating psychological strength
Weakness on sustained gradual climbs despite explosive power on steep short efforts
form_trajectory: neutral: Strong 2022-2023 Giro performances with multiple wins, but 2023 Vuelta showed positioning vulnerabilities and lack of GC contention. Role appears stage hunter rather than developing GC threat.
built_from: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 4
